title: Life as a locust officer: safeguarding crops and communities from desert locust
content: In this interview with PreventionWeb, Ali Babalifashki, Desert Locust Information Officer at the Plant Protection Organization of the Islamic Republic of Iran, shares insights from the field on managing locust risks and protecting vulnerable communities.

title: Evaluating the skill of AI and physics-based models for February 2020 North Atlantic storm hazards
content: In this study, the authors compare the performance of data-driven models to physics-based models in forecasting the February 2020 storm series over the United Kingdom.
snippet: In this study, the authors compare the performance of data-driven models to physics-based models in forecasting the February 2020 storm series over the United Kingdom.

title: Embedding risk finance in integrated national financing frameworks
content: This policy note explores how Integrated National Financing Frameworks (INFFs) can support more resilient financing by embedding climate risk diagnostics and climate disaster risk finance and insurance (CDRFI) mechanisms into national financing strategies
snippet: This policy note explores how Integrated National Financing Frameworks (INFFs) can support more resilient financing by embedding climate risk diagnostics and climate disaster risk finance and

title: Early warning! Early action! Early finance?
content: This special issue is about drawing lessons about recognising the importance of acting early, learning from practice, and strengthening the systems that make early action possible.
snippet: This special issue is about drawing lessons about recognising the importance of acting early, learning from practice, and strengthening the systems that make early action possible.

title: Using El Niño Impact Analysis to Inform Policy, Early Action and Risk-Informed Planning in Southern Africa
content: FAO’s El Niño work showed how combining multiple data sources, including agricultural losses and damages, sharpened early warning and made early action more targeted and effective across Southern Africa.

title: Climate finance and the governance challenge
content: Climate finance is often seen as a technical puzzle involving better proposals, stronger justifications, more readiness support, and more capacity building.  The assumption is straightforward: strengthen technical preparedness, and finance will follow.

title: Why climate and disaster risk governance must work together
content: In the century and a half after Tambora, disasters continued to take an enormous toll. Earthquakes, cyclones, and floods claimed hundreds of thousands of lives in multiple events, particularly in the first half of the twentieth century.

title: Pacific Prepared Podcast: ‘Learned a lot from my father and our ancestors’ Tongan resident explains the importance and relevance of traditional knowledge alongside modern technology
content: How the youngest female employee of the Tongan weather service is in love with her job and her advice is to people during quiet times with no disasters.
snippet: How the youngest female employee of the Tongan weather service is in love with her job and her advice is to people during quiet times with no disasters.

title: The Philippines is failing to include Indigenous people in climate decision making
content: Gap between principle and practice risks widening if the country does not more fully include Indigenous people in its climate action plan.
snippet: Gap between principle and practice risks widening if the country does not more fully include Indigenous people in its climate action plan.

title: Climate change exposes millions of Hajj pilgrims to deadly heat for increasingly longer parts of the year
content: Temperatures in Saudi Arabia are rising earlier in the year and reaching more dangerous extremes, making the pilgrimage increasingly hazardous not only in the summer months, where in 2024 more than 1300 pilgrims lost their lives.

title: Innovatek
content: Innovatek started in 1998 as a technology transfer event management company and has well over two decades within the industry.

title: Systemic climate adaptation framework
content: This report explores how, and to what degree, adaptation policies and governance arrangements are beginning to acknowledge and respond to the systemic nature of climate change.
snippet: This report explores how, and to what degree, adaptation policies and governance arrangements are beginning to acknowledge and respond to the systemic nature of climate change.

title: Brazil readiness assessment: Delivering anticipatory social protection
content: This study uses the ASPIRE tool to evaluate the readiness and effectiveness of social protection systems in Brazil, identifying gaps and opportunities to improve anticipatory measures and climate resilience.
snippet: This study uses the ASPIRE tool to evaluate the readiness and effectiveness of social protection systems in Brazil, identifying gaps and opportunities to improve anticipatory measures and climate

title: Worldwide job losses due to natural hazards
content: This study quantifies job losses due to floods, earthquakes, wind, storm surges, tsunamis, and heat across 132 countries, using a full-time job equivalent loss estimation approach.
snippet: This study quantifies job losses due to floods, earthquakes, wind, storm surges, tsunamis, and heat across 132 countries, using a full-time job equivalent loss estimation approach.

title: Natural hazards cost the world 90 million jobs every year
content: When we think about disasters, we often picture damaged buildings, flooded streets, or destroyed infrastructure. What is less visible, but just as devastating, is what happens to people’s jobs and livelihoods.

title: Scaling the dangers of dust storms
content: ASU-led team develops first large-scale ranking system to raise awareness about the dangers of Phoenix dust storms.
snippet: ASU-led team develops first large-scale ranking system to raise awareness about the dangers of Phoenix dust storms.

title: Study shows wetlands loss has increased residential flood insurance claim payments by $10 billion
content: By calculating the value of wetlands in reducing flood damage, findings can inform federal and state land use decisions, wetland protection, benefit-cost analysis and flood insurance policy.
snippet: By calculating the value of wetlands in reducing flood damage, findings can inform federal and state land use decisions, wetland protection, benefit-cost analysis and flood insurance policy.

title: Prepare for El Niño
content: A new WMO El Niño/La Niña Update indicates an 80% likelihood of an El Niño event during June–August 2026. Probabilities for this to continue until at least November are near or above 90%.
snippet: A new WMO El Niño/La Niña Update indicates an 80% likelihood of an El Niño event during June–August 2026. Probabilities for this to continue until at least November are near or above 90%.

title: Trees and greenery can cool cities by as much as 18°C – but only if they’re the right type
content: Cities around the world are planting more trees to cope with rising urban heat. But our research shows trees alone are often not enough.
snippet: Cities around the world are planting more trees to cope with rising urban heat. But our research shows trees alone are often not enough.

title: A simple — yet expensive — way to climate-proof the grid: Bury the power lines
content: Underground power lines are far less vulnerable to extreme weather, but burying them doesn't come cheap. After a historic ice storm in northern Michigan, utilities there are reconsidering the cost.
snippet: Underground power lines are far less vulnerable to extreme weather, but burying them doesn't come cheap. After a historic ice storm in northern Michigan, utilities there are reconsidering the cost.

title: How a monster ocean heatwave could fuel a super El Niño
content: The chance of an El Niño event emerging by July is now over 80 percent, which will likely make 2026 one of the hottest years on record.
snippet: The chance of an El Niño event emerging by July is now over 80 percent, which will likely make 2026 one of the hottest years on record.

title: Debugging: Google requests permission to release 32m mosquitoes in California and Florida
content: Company asks US government to release army of sterile male mosquitoes to lower number of illness-spreading bugs.
snippet: Company asks US government to release army of sterile male mosquitoes to lower number of illness-spreading bugs.

title: Krakatau and the architecture of vulnerability: Colonial governance, risk perception, and disaster impact
content: This article uses the 1883 eruption of Krakatau as a historical case study to examine how social vulnerability, colonial governance, and failures in risk communication affected the scale of disaster impacts.
snippet: This article uses the 1883 eruption of Krakatau as a historical case study to examine how social vulnerability, colonial governance, and failures in risk communication affected the scale of disaster

title: Greening Works, But Cities Must Plan It Smarter, states a new study from IIT Gandhinagar
content: <ul><li class="ck-list-marker-bold" data-list-item-id="e3e52ab3774ac103123830b53a1f0b51b"><strong>A study of 138 Indian cities combines satellite vegetation data, night-light data, extreme-aware heat downscaling, and explainable AI to show why urban greening must be matched to humidity, canopy density, and airflow.</strong></li></ul><p>Tree shade is one of the fastest ways to make heat more bearable. It cuts direct sunlight, protects people walking or working outdoors, and remains essential for Heat Action Plans.
snippet: A study of 138 Indian cities combines satellite vegetation data, night-light data, extreme-aware heat

title: 2026 Atlantic hurricane season begins: A strong El Niño could bring fewer storms
content: But “It only takes one storm to make for a very bad season,” said NOAA National Weather Service director Ken Graham.
snippet: But “It only takes one storm to make for a very bad season,” said NOAA National Weather Service director Ken Graham.

title: How extreme heat threatens honeybees
content: Hot temperatures can overwhelm bees’ natural cooling systems.
snippet: Hot temperatures can overwhelm bees’ natural cooling systems.

title: State Project Coordinator: INSPIRE India (Consultant) (Open for Indian Nationals)

title: ‘Before the rain came without warning. Now the message arrives before the clouds’
content: The farmer’s phone had vibrated just an hour earlier – a community alert of heavy rainfall expected within 48 hours. These messages now arrive regularly.
snippet: The farmer’s phone had vibrated just an hour earlier – a community alert of heavy rainfall expected within 48 hours. These messages now arrive regularly.

title: 2027 RISK Award "Homes at risk: Climate resilience for informal settlements"
content: Apply now for the 2027 RISK Award, endowed with a project funding of up to €100,000! Phase I runs from 1 June to 31 July 2026 (12:00 CET).

title: What is wet-bulb temperature and why does it matter?

title: Storm surge responses in Macau: Early warnings and soft adaptation
content: In this study, the authors investigated three major typhoons in Macau—Typhoons Hato (2017), Mangkhut (2018), and Ragasa (2025)—to examine changes in residents' evacuation behavior and government responses.

title: Hurricane season is here: Federal flood insurance carries 2 moral hazards – which you face depends largely on how wealthy you are
content: While federal flood insurance subsidizes risky coastal and waterfront development for wealthier homeowners by lowering the cost of living in these areas, many low-income households in flood-prone areas remain stuck with risky properties and little help.
snippet: While federal flood insurance subsidizes risky coastal and waterfront development for wealthier homeowners by lowering the cost of living in these areas, many low-income households in flood-prone

title: Leaving Home: They returned to Afghanistan, but not to stability
content: <p>In Pakistan, Khoja Gul was a businessman. He is Afghan, but like many around him, he moved across the border as a young man. There, he dealt in plastic scrap and owned a warehouse. He has a wife and eight children, and in his own words, they were “doing well in life.” Then, as the relationship between Pakistan and Afghanistan soured, the precarious life they had built collapsed around them.</p><p>They came home. Except the word home can mean many things, and for Gul it brings no comfort. Home means all ten of them squeezed together in a shanty in Kabul. “I have no job.
snippet: In Pakistan, Khoja Gul was a businessman. He is Afghan, but like many around him, he moved across the border as a young man. There, he dealt in plastic scrap and owned a warehouse. He has a wife

title: Leaving Home: She left after the fire; a Chilean community’s struggle to stay
content: <p>Carmen Mardones first began to call Canal Chacao home when she was 29. Set among the hills of Quilpué, the neighbourhood offered a life closer to the forest with their two young children: Kattya, four, and Jorge, two. Catalina, their third, was born after they had made the move, in 1997, from the nearby coastal city of Viña del Mar.</p><p>At first, she rented. Over time, she bought a house and spent years fixing it up. She continued to work in Viña del Mar, selling automobile parts in a business she had inherited from her father.

title: How capacity building helps climate adaptation last

title: Ebola may have spread beyond Africa. How are health authorities responding?
content: Without effective control measures, cases may surge in the coming months. Some models suggest that by mid-May, up to 1,000 cases had already occurred in the DRC, compared to official figures of about 900 cases.

title: Hailstorms could grow more dangerous and damaging with climate change
content: A study published in Nature predicts that climate change could produce larger, more damaging hailstones in some regions. The editors of Nature have also published a Research Briefing in the same issue summarizing the work.

title: Backlash is often swift when authorities try to plan retreat from the coast. There’s a better way
content: With climate change likely to accelerate extreme weather in Australia, planned or managed retreat – moving people and infrastructure away from these areas – will grow only more important.
snippet: With climate change likely to accelerate extreme weather in Australia, planned or managed retreat – moving people and infrastructure away from these areas – will grow only more important.

title: Cities are making it rain more – but not as much as scientists thought
content: Scientists think it really is raining more often over cities, but perhaps not as much as we thought.
snippet: Scientists think it really is raining more often over cities, but perhaps not as much as we thought.
